mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financing conditions, so increases in FEDFUNDS predict lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 5.12 for 2023-07-01, visible by 2023-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered walk-forward observations or if rolling 3-year Rank IC is above -0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation re-accelerates, investors price a longer period of restrictive policy and lower valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above its trailing 12-month growth rate while FEDFUNDS > 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value was 304.348 for 2023-07-01, visible by 2023-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered CPIAUCSL acceleration signal has a nonnegative 20-day forward return spread versus untriggered observations over two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with a low unemployment rate indicates resilient household income and low near-term recession risk, which supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE is below its trailing 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value was 156342.0 and UNRATE vintage value was 3.5 for 2023-07-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce a lower mean 20-day S&P 500 return than baseline observations with p <= 0.05 in the opposite direction, or if rolling 3-year hit rate falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in UNRATE signals rising recession probability and weakening labor income, causing equity investors to cut earnings forecasts and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE vintage value was 3.5 for 2023-07-01, visible by 2023-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases or if the triggered return mean is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real activity in retail sales and housing starts signals improving household demand and future corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change is positive and HOUST 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value was 696354.0 for 2023-07-01 and HOUST vintage value was 1434.0 for 2023-06-01
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive RSAFS-HOUST signal has a 60-day forward return hit rate below the unconditional positive-return rate over any 3-year rolling walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth indicates broad demand contraction and weaker aggregate profits, so declines in GDPC1 predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value was 20404.088 for 2023-04-01, visible by 2023-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if negative GDPC1 growth observations are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional negative-return frequency, or if mean triggered returns are not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in industrial production signals improving goods-sector output and operating leverage, leading analysts to revise cyclical earnings higher and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value was 102.2468 for 2023-06-01, visible by 2023-08-15
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO recoveries are followed by below-baseline 20-day S&P 500 returns in two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows or if Rank IC is less than 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A short-term collapse in housing starts signals tighter credit availability and weaker construction-linked demand, causing investors to reduce cyclical earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 1-month change crosses below -10% while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value was 1434.0 for 2023-06-01 and FEDFUNDS vintage value was 5.12 for 2023-07-01
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy hitting interest-sensitive demand
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST drops under high FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean.
